predicted_questions.question_text: Good morning. Two quick ones on the Europe/Med disruption: can you split the roughly 200 bps Q2 yield headwind you called out between (a) Mediterranean airlift/flight disruptions vs (b) West Coast Mexico demand effects and (c) dry-dock/timing items? And given you say bookings have been 'rebounding' in recent weeks, when should we expect Med yields/pricing to re-normalize toward the January trajectory — late Q3, Q4, or later?
predicted_questions.rationale: Matches the analyst's recurring focus on regional bookings/European occupancy (recurring_concerns.core_topics[0]) and requests the quarter-level magnitude and timing he typically presses for; ties directly to Naftali's comment that 'year‑over‑year comparison elements ... contribute almost 200 basis point headwind to yields' and management's note that Med bookings have recently been rebounding.

predicted_questions.question_text: Hey — on the Caribbean versus Europe divergence: you note Caribbean is 57% of deployment and that Legend will be redeployed plus Beach Club demand is 'very strong' — can you quantify the APD or yield premium you're seeing in the Caribbean versus the high‑yielding Mediterranean, and how much of the Caribbean outperformance is product‑pull (Beach Club/Legend) versus mix/new‑to‑cruise or pure pricing? Also, with you seeing more millennials/younger guests and higher pre‑cruise penetration, do you expect onboard spend per guest to continue to grow or should we expect some moderation as the mix shifts?
predicted_questions.rationale: Leverages the analyst's repeated probing of Caribbean vs Europe drivers (recurring_concerns.core_topics[1]) and onboard spend resilience (recurring_concerns.core_topics[2]), and asks for quantification and persistence tied to management remarks that Caribbean yields remain positive, Legend redeployment, strong Beach Club demand, increased younger guests, and higher pre‑cruise booking penetration.
